unit.name: Adaptive optimizers: RMSProp, Adam, and AdamW
unit.description: Introduces adaptive per-parameter scaling, Adam's combination of momentum and RMSProp, and decoupled weight decay in AdamW.
unit.summary: RMSProp is introduced as an adaptive optimizer that changes update scale by dividing by a decayed squared-gradient term [ts=2969s]. Its intuition is to take smaller steps in directions with large squared gradients and larger steps in flatter directions, so steep directions are damped and flat directions accelerated [ts=3039s]. The visual comparison shows RMSProp turning toward the valley center more quickly than SGD because it changes the relative step sizes by dimension [ts=3159s]. Adam is described as roughly RMSProp with momentum: it combines a first-moment velocity with a second-moment squared-gradient estimate [ts=3226s]. A naive Adam form has a first-timestep issue because moment estimates start at zero and beta values such as 0.9 and 0.999 are close to 1, so the full Adam form adds bias correction [ts=3305s]. Adam with beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, and learning rate 1e-3 or 5e-4 is recommended as a strong starting point for many models [ts=3403s]. AdamW decouples weight decay: standard Adam includes L2 in moment calculations, while AdamW applies decay after computing moments so velocity reflects the loss landscape more independently of raw weight values [ts=3457s].

key_points.text: RMSProp adds element-wise scaling based on historical squared gradients with decay.
RMSProp dampens steep directions and accelerates flatter directions.
Adam combines momentum-style first moments with RMSProp-style second moments.
Adam needs bias correction because moment estimates start at zero.
A common Adam starting point is beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, and learning rate 1e-3 or 5e-4.
Standard Adam includes L2 in moment calculations, while AdamW applies weight decay after moments are computed.

kp_catalog.name: Momentum optimizer
kp_catalog.description: Using velocity or a running average of gradients to smooth updates and keep moving in useful directions.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because momentum optimizer clarifies using velocity or a running average of gradients to smooth updates and keep moving in useful directions, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: RMSProp and adaptive learning rates
kp_catalog.description: Element-wise update scaling by historical squared gradients, giving per-parameter adaptive learning rates.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because rMSProp and adaptive learning rates clarifies element-wise update scaling by historical squared gradients, giving per-parameter adaptive learning rates, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Adam and AdamW
kp_catalog.description: Adam combines momentum and RMSProp-style adaptivity; AdamW decouples weight decay from moment estimation.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because adam and AdamW formalizes how adam combines momentum and RMSProp-style adaptivity; AdamW decouples weight decay from moment estimation, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
